metadata.title: The Infinity Loop: What Endless Games Are Actually Designing
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Seth Brown
tags: The Infinity Loop: What Endless Games Ar
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Seth Brown — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. A new release feeling expected is ordinary; most games never build a true endless loop. Studios did not create the value players find in these systems. Instead they created the conditions under which that value emerges. Endless games are really designing the loop that lets players generate meaning themselves.

artifactLines: A new release feeling expected is ordinary; most games never build a true endless loop.
Studios did not create the value players find in these systems.
Instead they created the conditions under which that value emerges.
Endless games are really designing the loop that lets players generate meaning themselves.

beats.narration_text: The idea that studios don't create value, they create the conditions for it, is the thread I want to pull on for endless games. I want to design a small loop that manufactures those conditions. Help me reason it through. Give me: (1) what specifically an infinity loop is designing for beyond content, (2) the mechanics that let players generate value studios only enabled, (3) one failure mode where the loop feels like a treadmill instead.
